message:
Align gen filename on capnwasm/vite-plugin convention (.capnp.gen.mjs)

The plugin emits <basename-with-ext>.gen.mjs (so users.capnp produces
users.capnp.gen.mjs). The CLI in package.json was writing
users.gen.mjs, which meant the plugin's output and the CLI's output
landed in different files and the imports went stale.

Renamed the codegen target + the two import sites:

  scripts/generate-fixtures.mjs   imports users.capnp.gen.mjs
  src/playground/main.ts          imports users.capnp.gen.mjs
  package.json                    `npm run codegen` writes the same

Now the prepare-step CLI codegen and the dev-mode plugin emit the
same file, in the same place. .gitignore already covers
src/**/*.gen.mjs from the previous commit so the file stays
untracked and never drifts.

files.patch:
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
   "private": true,
   "type": "module",
   "scripts": {
-    "codegen": "node ../bin/capnwasm.mjs gen users.capnp -o src/playground/users.gen.mjs",
+    "codegen": "node ../bin/capnwasm.mjs gen users.capnp -o src/playground/users.capnp.gen.mjs",
     "prepare": "cp ../dist/capnp.slim.wasm public/ && npm run codegen && node scripts/generate-fixtures.mjs",
     "dev": "npm run prepare && vite",
     "build": "npm run prepare && vite build",
